items.name: Fairy Slingshot
items.from: Treasure chest inside the Great Deku Tree (your first dungeon, as young Link).
items.what: A child's ranged weapon that fires Deku Seeds. Stuns small enemies and hits faraway switches, eyes, and ropes.
items.tip: Shoot the spiked ladder-ropes and eye switches in the Deku Tree; later snipe the eye above doors to open them.

items.name: Bomb Bag (Bombs)
items.from: Big treasure chest in Dodongo's Cavern (holds 20 bombs; upgradable to 30 and 40 via the Hot Rodder Goron in Goron City and the Bombchu Bowling Alley).
items.what: Lets you carry and throw Bombs that blast cracked walls/boulders, expose secrets, and damage enemies.
items.tip: Bomb cracked walls everywhere — many Great Fairy fountains, grottos, and shortcuts hide behind them.

items.name: Bombchu
items.from: Found in dungeon chests, or buy from the Bombchu Shop in the Back Alley of Hyrule Castle Town (child only, open at night; you need the Adult's Wallet to afford a pack).
items.what: A wind-up mouse bomb that drives along floors, walls, and ceilings before exploding — reaches what a thrown bomb can't.
items.tip: Send one up a wall or across a gap to hit a switch or cracked wall you can't reach by hand.

items.name: Fairy Bow
items.from: Big treasure chest in the Forest Temple (adult Link's first dungeon).
items.what: The adult bow. Fires arrows for long-range damage and hits distant switches, eye targets, and weak points.
items.tip: Shoot the twisting-corridor and painting puzzles in the Forest Temple; later the base for Fire, Ice, and Light Arrows.

items.name: Fire Arrows
items.from: Lake Hylia, as adult: stand on the plaque/platform above the Water Temple entrance and shoot the sun the moment it rises.
items.what: Arrows wreathed in flame that ignite torches, burn webs and ice, and deal extra fire damage.
items.tip: Light far-off torches from a distance, and melt ice obstacles in the Ice Cavern and Water Temple area.

items.name: Ice Arrows
items.from: Prize for clearing the Gerudo Training Ground (Gerudo's Fortress, adult).
items.what: Arrows that freeze enemies solid on hit. Largely optional — not needed to finish the game.
items.tip: Freeze a tough enemy in place to line up a follow-up hit; handy in the Training Ground itself.

items.name: Light Arrows
items.from: Given by Princess Zelda (revealed from Sheik) at the Temple of Time after you clear the five adult temples and awaken the Sages.
items.what: Holy arrows of pure light that devastate evil enemies — the key weapon against Ganondorf and Ganon.
items.tip: Stun Ganondorf with a Light Arrow during his fight, then close in with the sword.

items.name: Boomerang
items.from: Treasure chest inside Jabu-Jabu's Belly (young Link only).
items.what: A throwing weapon that stuns enemies, cuts ropes, and grabs distant items/switches before returning to you.
items.tip: Stun the jellyfish-like enemies and hit far switches in Jabu-Jabu; great for snagging out-of-reach pickups.

items.name: Hookshot
items.from: Win the gravedigging race against Dampé's ghost in the Kakariko Graveyard (adult Link).
items.what: A chain-launched grapple that pulls Link to wooden targets/posts and yanks distant enemies and items toward him.
items.tip: Grapple to tree trunks, signs, and treasure to cross gaps; stun and reel in enemies from afar.

items.name: Longshot
items.from: Treasure chest in the Water Temple, in the room where you fight Dark Link.
items.what: An upgraded Hookshot with roughly twice the reach — same grapple/pull function, much greater range.
items.tip: Use the extra reach for far targets the Hookshot can't touch, including the Biggoron's Sword trade and Water Temple puzzles.

items.name: Megaton Hammer
items.from: Treasure chest in the Fire Temple (adult), after freeing the trapped Gorons.
items.what: A massive hammer that smashes rusted switches and cracked black stone, and pounds armored foes flat.
items.tip: Pound the rusted switches in the Fire Temple, and shatter the red boulders that block Death Mountain Crater paths.

items.name: Lens of Truth
items.from: Treasure chest at the Bottom of the Well in Kakariko (young Link), after defeating Dead Hand.
items.what: Reveals invisible things — hidden enemies, fake walls, secret platforms and chests — while it drains Magic.
items.tip: Essential in the Shadow Temple: hold it up to expose invisible bridges and walk across the empty-looking gaps.

items.name: Magic Beans
items.from: Buy from the Bean Seller at Zora's River (10 rupees for the first, rising by 10 each up to 100 for the tenth).
items.what: Plant one in patches of soft soil as young Link; as an adult, each sprouts into a leafy platform that lifts or carries you.
items.tip: Plant beans in soft soil during childhood, then ride the grown plants as an adult to reach Heart Pieces and high ledges.

items.name: Din's Fire
items.from: Great Fairy Fountain on the Hyrule Castle ledge (bomb the corner rock, crawl in) — young Link.
items.what: A spell that bursts a ring of fire around Link, hitting all nearby enemies and lighting every torch at once.
items.tip: Stand on an unlit torch puzzle and cast once to light them all together; also clears swarms of close enemies.

items.name: Farore's Wind
items.from: Great Fairy Fountain behind a bombable wall in Zora's Fountain.
items.what: A spell that sets a warp point inside a dungeon; cast again to teleport back to it — only works in dungeons with a map.
items.tip: Drop a warp at a dungeon's start before a long detour so a death or wrong turn doesn't cost you the whole trek.

items.name: Nayru's Love
items.from: Great Fairy Fountain behind a bombable wall at the Desert Colossus (near the Spirit Temple).
items.what: A spell that surrounds Link in a crystal shield, blocking all damage (you can still be knocked back) until Magic runs out.
items.tip: Cast it before a tough boss or the Iron Knuckles for a window of total invincibility.

items.name: Empty Bottle
items.from: Four total: Lon Lon Ranch Super Cucco game, the Kakariko Cucco Lady, the Ruto's-Letter bottle underwater at Lake Hylia, and the 10 Big Poes reward.
items.what: All-purpose containers. A bottle can hold fairies, potions (red/green/blue), Lon Lon Milk, bugs, fish, Blue Fire, Poes, or fairy souls.
items.tip: Always carry a captured fairy — if Link dies with one bottled, it revives him with 8 hearts automatically.

items.name: Iron Boots
items.from: Treasure chest in the Ice Cavern (adult), beyond Zora's Domain.
items.what: Heavy boots that sink Link underwater so he can walk along the bottom; also anchor him against wind and pull.
items.tip: Equip them to descend and explore the flooded floors of the Water Temple; swap them off to float back up.

items.name: Hover Boots
items.from: Treasure chest in the Shadow Temple (adult), after defeating Dead Hand.
items.what: Boots that let Link skate briefly across thin air after he steps off a ledge — a short hover with no traction.
items.tip: Run off an edge and drift across a gap that's too wide to jump in the Shadow Temple and Ganon's Castle.

items.name: Goron Tunic
items.from: Free from Link the Goron in Goron City (adult), or buy it at the Goron Shop for 200 rupees.
items.what: A red tunic that protects Link from extreme heat, letting him survive Death Mountain Crater and the Fire Temple.
items.tip: Wear it before entering the Fire Temple or the Crater — without it the heat drains your hearts.

items.name: Zora Tunic
items.from: Free from King Zora after you thaw him with Blue Fire (adult), or buy it at the Zora Shop for 300 rupees.
items.what: A blue tunic that lets Link breathe underwater indefinitely, so he can swim and dive without drowning.
items.tip: Equip it before the Water Temple so you never have to surface for air mid-puzzle.

items.name: Silver Gauntlets
items.from: Treasure chest in the Spirit Temple after defeating the Iron Knuckle (reachable as young Link via the Requiem of Spirit).
items.what: Strength-boosting gauntlets that let Link lift heavy silver/black blocks he otherwise couldn't budge.
items.tip: Push the big block at the Spirit Temple entrance to open the path forward and unite the child/adult halves.

items.name: Golden Gauntlets
items.from: Treasure chest in Ganon's Castle (the Shadow/silver-barrier area), as adult Link.
items.what: The ultimate strength upgrade — lets Link lift and hurl enormous pillars far beyond the Silver Gauntlets' limit.
items.tip: Use them to heave the giant black pillar blocking the way in Ganon's Castle.

items.name: Mirror Shield
items.from: Big treasure chest in the Spirit Temple (adult half).
items.what: A polished shield that reflects light and magical beams back at their source — a puzzle and combat tool.
items.tip: Catch sunlight and aim the beam onto sun-faced statues and frozen Stalfos to open paths and stun foes.

items.name: Hylian Shield
items.from: Buy at the Castle Town Bazaar for ~80 rupees, or grab it free from a flower-marked grave in the Kakariko Graveyard at night.
items.what: The big knight's shield. Adult Link wields it normally; it blocks attacks the small Deku/wooden shields can't, and it won't burn.
items.tip: Equip it for the adult game — it deflects strong projectiles and survives fire that destroys the Deku Shield.

items.name: Biggoron's Sword
items.from: Reward for finishing the long Biggoron trade quest (Cucco Lady → ... → World's Finest Eye Drops → Claim Check), redeemed at Biggoron on Death Mountain.
items.what: A two-handed great sword that hits twice as hard as the Master Sword and — unlike the Giant's Knife — never breaks.
items.tip: Swing it two-handed for big damage on bosses, but remember you can't hold a shield while it's drawn.

items.name: Magic Meter
items.from: The base meter is a gift from the Great Fairy at Death Mountain's summit (with the Spin Attack); Double Magic comes from the Great Fairy in Death Mountain Crater (behind boulders broken with the Megaton Hammer).
items.what: Your green Magic Power bar — the fuel for the Slingshot's special, the three spells, Fire/Light Arrows, and the Lens of Truth.
items.tip: Refill it with Magic Jars or a Green Potion; Double Magic doubles the bar so you can cast far more before running dry.

items.name: Ocarina of Time
items.from: Start with the Fairy Ocarina (Saria gives it as you leave Kokiri Forest); upgraded to the Ocarina of Time when Princess Zelda tosses it to you as she flees the castle with Impa.
items.what: The titular instrument. Play learned songs on it to warp across Hyrule, change day to night, open the Door of Time, summon storms, and trigger countless puzzles.
items.tip: Learn the Sun's Song early to flip day/night on demand, and grab the warp songs to fast-travel between regions.

items.name: Zelda's Lullaby
items.from: Impa, just outside Hyrule Castle (after meeting Princess Zelda)
items.what: The song of Hyrule's Royal Family. Played at Triforce crests, it opens doors and triggers events.
items.tip: Play it on Triforce symbols to unblock progress (e.g. the Goron City crest, Zora's Domain waterfall) and to enter the Royal Family's Tomb in the graveyard.

items.name: Saria's Song
items.from: Saria, deep in the Sacred Forest Meadow (Lost Woods)
items.what: Magically contacts Saria, who gives a hint about what to do next. Also pleases certain characters.
items.tip: Play it to get a free next-step hint anytime you're lost; also needed to win over the Goron leader Darunia in Goron City.

items.name: Epona's Song
items.from: Malon at Lon Lon Ranch (talk to her, then pull out the ocarina)
items.what: Summons the horse Epona to your side; also fills empty bottles with Lon Lon Milk and calms her.
items.tip: As an adult, learn it to call Epona and jump the ranch fences to escape with your horse.

items.name: Sun's Song
items.from: Found in the Royal Family's Tomb, Kakariko Graveyard (read the inscription; enter via Zelda's Lullaby)
items.what: Instantly toggles between day and night; also freezes ReDeads and Gibdos in place.
items.tip: Use it to skip to the time you need (shops, ghosts, gravediggers) and to paralyze ReDeads before they grab you.

items.name: Song of Time
items.from: Princess Zelda (taught via vision after she tosses you the Ocarina of Time at the castle drawbridge)
items.what: Opens the Door of Time, and makes blue Time Blocks appear or vanish.
items.tip: Required to open the Door of Time and claim the Master Sword; replay it on blue blocks to solve dungeon puzzles.

items.name: Song of Storms
items.from: Guru-Guru, the windmill man, inside the Kakariko Village windmill (as an adult)
items.what: Summons rain. Played at certain spots it fills wells, drains the windmill, exposes grottos, or refills a Magic Bean spot.
items.tip: Play it as a child at the bottom of the Kakariko well to drain it, opening the path to the Bottom of the Well dungeon.

items.name: Minuet of Forest
items.from: Sheik, at the entrance to the Sacred Forest Meadow (adult)
items.what: Warp song. Teleports you to the Sacred Forest Meadow, right by the Forest Temple entrance.
items.tip: Use it to fast-travel straight to the Forest Temple instead of crossing the Lost Woods.

items.name: Bolero of Fire
items.from: Sheik, in Death Mountain Crater (reached via the Hookshot over the broken bridge)
items.what: Warp song. Teleports you to Death Mountain Crater, near the Fire Temple entrance.
items.tip: Warp here to reach the Fire Temple instantly and skip the long climb up Death Mountain.

items.name: Serenade of Water
items.from: Sheik, in the Ice Cavern after you grab the Iron Boots
items.what: Warp song. Teleports you to Lake Hylia, beside the entrance to the Water Temple.
items.tip: Warp here to reach the Water Temple; pair the trip with the Iron Boots and Zora Tunic you'll need inside.

items.name: Nocturne of Shadow
items.from: Sheik, outside Kakariko Village (cutscene after clearing the Water Temple)
items.what: Warp song. Teleports you to the Kakariko Graveyard, by the Shadow Temple entrance.
items.tip: Learn it by returning to a burning Kakariko after the Water Temple, then warp here to start the Shadow Temple.

items.name: Requiem of Spirit
items.from: Sheik, at the Desert Colossus (your first visit, after crossing the Haunted Wasteland)
items.what: Warp song. Teleports you to the Desert Colossus, in front of the Spirit Temple.
items.tip: Once learned, warp straight to the Colossus so you never have to re-cross the Haunted Wasteland.

items.name: Prelude of Light
items.from: Sheik, at the Temple of Time (after you complete the Forest Temple)
items.what: Warp song. Teleports you to the Temple of Time in Hyrule Castle Town.
items.tip: Use it to return to the Master Sword pedestal to switch between child and adult Link.
